# wordpress_plugin (Resource)

Manages a Wordpress plugin. Creating this resource will install the plugin, and deleting it will uninstall the plugin. The plugin can be activated or deactivated by setting the `status` attribute to `active` or `inactive`.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `slug` (String) The slug of the plugin. This is used to identify the plugin in WordPress and to install it. For example, the slug for the Hello Dolly plugin is `hello-dolly`

### Optional

- `status` (String) The status of the plugin. Can be 'active' or 'inactive'.

### Read-Only

- `author_uri` (String)
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `name` (String)
- `network_only` (Boolean)
- `plugin` (String)
- `plugin_uri` (String)
- `requires_php` (String)
- `requires_wp` (String)
- `textdomain` (String)
- `version` (String)
